summary: Tested CorteQS Diaspora Globe full-stack. 6/13 backend tests pass (3 skipped due to chained failures). Public APIs work (health, /pins returns 25 approved pins, /geocode, /auth/me 401, /auth/logout, signup). CRITICAL: every authenticated endpoint (login, /auth/me with token, POST /pins, /pins/admin, PATCH /pins) returns 500/401 due to Supabase RLS policy violation on `profiles` table when current_user() upserts the profile. This blocks login, pin creation, and the entire admin flow. Frontend renders the 3D globe, search bar, filter rail, FAB, auth modal correctly, but pin count shows 0 in the rail/footer (possible delayed render or pins not propagating into state visibly). Google OAuth not tested (browser-redirect, not safe in headless).

critical.issue: Returns 500. RLS error: 'new row violates row-level security policy for table profiles' from upsert_profile() in current_user(). Service role client does not bypass RLS — likely the table has FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY or service_role policies are missing in supabase_setup.sql.

Returns 401 'Invalid credentials' even for the freshly-signed-up valid user. Root cause: login() upserts profile after sign_in_with_password and the broad `except Exception` swallows the RLS error, masquerading it as 401. Two bugs combined: (a) RLS upsert failure, (b) overly broad except that misclassifies all errors as bad creds.
Returns 500 — same RLS error in current_user → upsert_profile chain.
Returns 500 instead of 403 when non-admin user calls — same RLS upsert blocks before role check runs.
Same — would 500 instead of 403 because current_user fails first.

minor.issue: Catches all exceptions and returns 401. Should at least log + reraise non-auth errors as 500 with detail.
Calls upsert_profile on every authenticated request. Even after RLS fix this is wasteful — profile already exists post-signup. Should only upsert if profile not found.

ui_bugs.issue: Filter chip 'Hepsi' shows count 0 and footer shows '0 pin · tüm kategoriler' on initial load even though /api/pins returns 25 pins. No pin emojis visible on the globe in screenshot. Need to verify whether pins eventually render (possible async timing) or if there is a state propagation bug.

integration_issues.issue: Could not test end-to-end via UI because backend signupEmail succeeds but immediate refresh()→/auth/me fails with 500 (RLS). User would remain logged-out visually after signup.

issues: Visually clean — globe, search, filter rail, FAB layout look good. Turkish copy correct. No major design issues observed.

action_items: FIX RLS for service_role on `profiles` table — update /app/backend/supabase_setup.sql to grant service_role full access OR disable FORCE RLS. Service role is supposed to bypass RLS but the current policies on profiles block UPSERT. Add policy: `CREATE POLICY "service_role full access" ON profiles FOR ALL TO service_role USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);` OR better, ensure `ALTER TABLE profiles FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` is NOT used.

ALTERNATIVE FIX (code-side): change current_user() to only call upsert_profile when profile does not already exist (SELECT first). Profile is already created at signup/login, so per-request upsert is unnecessary. This avoids the RLS write path on every request.
Fix login() error handling — don't catch all exceptions as 401. Catch supabase.AuthApiError specifically; let other errors propagate as 500.
Investigate frontend pin count showing 0 while backend returns 25 — verify api.get('/pins') in App.loadPins, check Network tab, and check whether setPins is being called.
After RLS fix, retest admin flow: append test user email to ADMIN_EMAILS in /app/backend/.env, restart backend, login again, hit /api/pins/admin, PATCH and DELETE.
Save test credentials to /app/memory/test_credentials.md once auth is unblocked.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py current_user(): unconditional upsert_profile on every request — both a perf concern and the cause of the RLS 500s. Should be read-only or upsert-on-miss.
server.py login(): bare `except Exception` masks all errors as 'Invalid credentials' — bad UX/debug.
server.py: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is read at module import via os.environ[...] (KeyError on missing) — OK but no graceful fail message.
supabase_setup.sql not reviewed (file referenced but RLS policies appear too restrictive).

AuthContext.signupEmail: relies on backend to return access_token+refresh_token then sets supabase session client-side — works only if signup endpoint succeeds; refresh() afterwards will currently fail (500) due to RLS.
logout sets samesite='none' + secure=True — requires HTTPS (OK in prod preview).

success_rate.frontend: Smoke OK; pin rendering count discrepancy not root-caused

test_credentials: Created during this run: testdiaspora_<TS>@example.com / test123456 (signup succeeded, but login currently fails due to RLS bug — credentials not yet usable). Did NOT update /app/memory/test_credentials.md because the user is not currently usable.
seed_data_creation: Confirmed 25 sample pins already seeded and visible via GET /api/pins. No new seed data created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the RLS issue (either via SQL policy update for service_role OR by removing the per-request upsert in current_user), re-run /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — all 13 tests should pass except the admin-positive flow which requires editing ADMIN_EMAILS. The critical path to verify: POST /auth/login returns 200 with token, GET /auth/me with Bearer returns user, POST /pins returns 200 pending pin, GET /pins/admin without admin returns 403 (not 500). Then run frontend Playwright to verify signup → user-chip appears → Pin ekle → AddPinModal → submit → toast.
rca_of_the_issue: RCA: Backend log shows postgrest APIError code 42501 ('new row violates row-level security policy for table "profiles"') on every upsert. The service role client (sb=create_client(URL, SERVICE_ROLE_KEY)) is correctly initialized but the Supabase project's RLS policies on `profiles` are not allowing service_role writes. Reproduction: signup the test user → call GET /api/pins/mine with the returned access_token → 500. Mitigation: (a) add a service_role bypass policy to profiles in supabase_setup.sql and re-run in Supabase SQL Editor, OR (b) refactor current_user() to skip upsert when profile already exists. Login's broad except converts the 500 to 401, masking the real bug — fix the except too.
